title: Share of deaths from suicide
subtitle: Estimated share of deaths from suicide. Suicides are underreported in many countries due to social stigma and cultural or legal concerns. caption:
Share of deaths from suicide

Estimated share of deaths from suicide. Suicides are underreported in many countries due to social stigma and cultural or legal concerns. This data is based on modeling of global suicide patterns, and includes adjustments and extrapolations that aim to account for missing data and underreporting.

This line chart tracks the estimated share of deaths from suicide for seven countries from 1980 to 2023, with the vertical axis ranging from 0% to over 6%. South Korea displays the most significant fluctuation, starting near 2.3% in 1980, rising steadily to a peak above 6% around 2010, and subsequently declining to approximately 4% by 2023. The United States line begins near 1.5% and trends upward to just under 2% by the end of the period. Japan starts above 3% in 1980, peaks near 3.6% in the mid-1980s, and follows a general downward trajectory to finish near 1.6%. Germany and the United Kingdom maintain relatively stable paths in the lower-middle range, hovering between 1% and 1.6% throughout the timeline. Peru and Greece occupy the lowest positions on the chart; Peru rises from near 0.2% to roughly 0.7%, while Greece remains consistently below 0.5%. The data reflects modeled estimates intended to adjust for underreporting and missing data.
